text: Topology diagram shows task submission going to Hugin, but clients submit to Munin; Hugin only exposes /health
Stale-task recovery description says 'time in running state' but code measures time since submission
Deployment section says .env is deployed and only built artifacts synced; actual script syncs repo tree and preserves .env on target
Internal inconsistency: 'all three services' vs 'two-service system'
'Three src/index.ts files' claim is rhetoric; important behavior lives in munin-client.ts, systemd units, deploy scripts
Missing rationale for markdown task format over JSON/typed schema
Missing rationale for polling+search as queue primitive vs dedicated queue
Missing rationale for tags as state machine vs structured fields
'One task at a time' justified only with slogan, not actual rationale (CPU? isolation? cost?)
4000-char output truncation has no stated rationale or discussion of what's lost
No rationale for full CLI spawning vs narrower execution sandbox, inconsistent with security posture
Document lacks source-of-truth pointers; Munin/Mimir sections presented as authoritative but code lives elsewhere
Guide evaluated as 'well-written' but trustworthiness is the key criterion for a reference document
Stale-recovery fix should resolve code-vs-design mismatch, not just update prose
Action items are document-centric; should prioritize trustworthiness pass over polish pass
